recipes.title: Orange & Ginger Rye Scones
recipes.description: Earthy rye scones flavored with fresh orange zest and grated ginger, finished with a professional turbinado sugar crust.
ingredients: 1/2 cup (1 stick) Unsalted Butter, cold and cubed
1/2 cup Dried Cranberries OR Macadamia Nuts
steps: Freeze the Butter: Cube butter and freeze for 10 minutes before starting.
Mix Dry Ingredients: Whisk flours, sugar, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl.
Incorporate Butter: Rub cold butter into flour until it looks like coarse crumbs with some pea-sized chunks.
Add Aromatics: Stir in the orange zest, grated ginger, and optional inclusions.
Whisk Wet Ingredients: Combine heavy cream, egg, vanilla, and orange juice.
Combine: Pour wet into dry. Stir with a fork until a shaggy dough forms. Do not overwork.
Shape: Pat dough into an 8-inch disc (about 1 inch thick) on a floured surface.
Cut: Cut into 8 wedges and place on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Finish: Brush tops with extra cream and sprinkle generously with Turbinado sugar.
Bake: Bake at 400°F for 18–22 minutes until edges are golden brown.
recipes.notes: Secret to success: Keep the butter cold until it hits the oven. If your kitchen is warm, chill the shaped scones for 15 minutes before baking.

recipes.title: Banana Oat Power Muffins
recipes.description: A sophisticated evolution of the classic breakfast bar, using Rye Flour and Sour Cream for a deeper flavor and moist crumb.
ingredients: Topping: 1/2 cup oats, 1/4 tsp cinnamon, 2 tbsp brown sugar, 2 tbsp melted butter
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F and line or grease a 12-cup muffin tin.
Whisk Wet Ingredients: Combine bananas, eggs, melted butter, sour cream, brown sugar, and vanilla.
Mix Dry Ingredients: Whisk flours, oats, leaveners, salt, and nutmeg in a separate bowl.
Combine: Gently fold dry ingredients into wet using a spatula. Do not overmix.
Fold in Extras: Stir in chocolate chips, nuts, and seed mix until just incorporated.
Bake: Divide batter into 12 cups and bake for 20–22 minutes until golden.
Cool: Allow to cool in the pan for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack.
recipes.notes: The acidity in sour cream reacts with the baking soda to create a better lift for the muffin domes.

recipes.title: Brown Butter & Garden Herb Chicken
recipes.description: Chicken cutlets flavored with garden herbs and a brown butter lemon sauce, served over creamy Parmesan polenta.
steps: The Polenta: Boil liquid, whisk in polenta. Simmer 15-20 mins. Stir in Parmesan and butter.
Herb Rub: Chop Thyme and Oregano. Season dry chicken cutlets with herbs, salt, and pepper.
Sear: Sauté chicken 3-4 mins per side until golden. Remove and tent with foil.
Brown Butter: Melt 2 tbsp butter. Fry Sage, Rosemary, and Garlic until butter turns nutty amber.
Deglaze: Add lemon juice and broth to stop the browning. Scrape the pan for bits.
Reduce: Let sauce bubble and reduce by half. Add chicken back to coat in glossy butter.
Serve: Bed of polenta topped with chicken and crispy herbs.
recipes.notes: Pantry Pairing: Sauté Broccolini in the same pan with garlic and lemon after the chicken is done.

recipes.title: Beef & Lentil Cottage Pie
recipes.description: A savory ground beef and lentil filling topped with a marbled Japanese sweet potato and purple potato mash.
steps: Prepare Mash: Boil potatoes 15-20 mins. Mash with cream and butter. Keep marbled or smooth.
Cook Filling: Brown beef in an oven-safe skillet. Sauté diced carrots and celery.
Simmer: Add lentils, herbs, and broth. Simmer 20-25 mins until lentils absorb liquid into gravy.
Assemble: Spread mash over beef mixture. Use a fork to create ridges.
Bake: Bake at 400°F for 10-15 mins or broil for 3-5 mins until cheese is golden.
Sauté Greens: Steam broccolini in broth, then sauté with butter, lemon, and oregano.
recipes.notes: Japanese sweet potatoes and purple potatoes create a beautiful 'marbled' look for the topping.

recipes.title: Crispy Miso-Ginger Tofu
recipes.description: Crispy cornstarch-coated tofu glazed in a rich miso-ginger sauce with charred vegetables over jasmine rice.
steps: Start the Rice: Rinse and cook Jasmine rice according to package instructions.
Prep Tofu: Toss cubed tofu in cornstarch and salt until chalky.
Sear: Fry tofu cubes in oil until golden and crispy (8-10 mins). Set aside.
Veggies: Sauté broccolini and carrots. Steam for 2 mins with water, then sauté to char.
Glaze: Whisk miso, ginger, garlic, soy, honey, vinegar, and chili sauce.
Combine: Add tofu back to pan with veggies. Pour glaze over and toss for 30-60 seconds.
Serve: Bed of rice topped with glazed tofu and fresh green onions.
recipes.notes: Ensure tofu is pressed and patted very dry before coating in cornstarch for maximum crispiness.

recipes.title: Banana Breakfast Bars
recipes.description: Hearty, nutrient-dense breakfast bars packed with oats, nuts, and seeds.
ingredients: 1/4 cup Sliced Almonds or Macadamia nuts
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F and line a 9x13 pan with parchment.
Dry Base: Combine flours, oats, soda, salt, nutmeg, brown sugar, and shredded coconut.
Wet Base: Whisk mashed bananas, syrup, vanilla, oil, and eggs until smooth.
Combine: Pour wet ingredients into dry and mix until just combined.
Fold in Extras: Gently fold in toasted pecans, dried berries, nuts, and the seed mix.
Bake: Spread evenly in pan and bake 25-30 minutes until set.
Cool: Allow to cool completely in the pan before slicing into bars.
recipes.notes: These freeze beautifully! Store at room temperature for 3 days or refrigerate for a week.

recipes.title: Moist Banana Bread
recipes.description: A zero-frills recipe that delivers big on banana flavor. Insanely moist due to the oil base and extra-ripe bananas.
steps: Preheat oven to 325°F. Grease an 8x5 inch loaf pan and line with parchment.
Dry Mix: Whisk together flour, baking soda, salt, and nutmeg.
Wet Mix: Whisk eggs, oil, sugar, and vanilla until smooth. Stir in mashed bananas.
Combine: Mix dry ingredients into wet until no streaks remain. Do not overmix.
Bake: Transfer to pan and bake 60–70 minutes until toothpick comes out clean.
Finish: Immediately sprinkle the top with Turbinado sugar while hot.
Cool: Cool in pan for 20 minutes, then lift out to cool completely on a rack.
recipes.notes: Use bananas that are nearly black and syrup-y in texture for the best moisture and flavor.

recipes.title: Simply Sandwich Bread
recipes.description: Soft and buttery with a chewy/crisp crust, this fresh loaf of bread is a kitchen staple.
ingredients: 3 1/3 cups (425g) AP Flour or Bread Flour
steps: Prepare the dough: Whisk warm water, milk, yeast, and sugar. Let sit for 5 minutes.
Mix: Add butter, salt, and 1 cup flour. Beat on low. Add another cup, beat on medium. Add remaining flour until dough pulls away from sides.
Knead: Knead in mixer for 5-8 mins (or by hand) until soft, tacky, and it bounces back when poked.
1st Rise: Place in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm spot for 1-2 hours until doubled.
Shape: Punch dough down. Roll into an 8x15 inch rectangle, then roll into an 8-inch log.
2nd Rise: Place log in a greased 9x5 pan. Cover and rise until 1 inch above the pan rim (about 1 hour).
Bake: Bake at 350°F on a lower rack for 32-36 minutes until golden brown (195-200°F internal).
Cool: Allow to cool in pan for a few minutes before transferring to a wire rack.
recipes.notes: Bread flour produces a chewier loaf. To freeze, shape the loaf and freeze in the pan for up to 3 months; thaw and rise for 5 hours before baking.
